title: ruellia silvaccola
intro: Ruellia silvaccola, also known as forest ruellia, is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ruellia silvaccola include its potential to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
